GC-CLOUD WAVE 4 — MONTHLY STATUS REPORT
Reporting Period: February 2026
Report Date: March 1, 2026
Project Manager: Carol Nguyen
Status: GREEN

---

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

GC-Cloud Wave 4 is progressing excellently. The team has made strong
progress across all workstreams and stakeholder engagement remains high.
We are confident in our ability to deliver the project on schedule and
within the approved parameters.

---

extracted_tasks.text: will be submitted to the Director for consideration.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: will be submitted to the Director for consideration.

risks.description: A $340,000 cost overrun (approximately 14% above the approved $2,400,000 envelope) has been identified due to unscoped middleware remediation requirements. The budget revision has not yet been approved, and the project is proceeding without confirmed funding, creating a material budget risk that is downplayed in the executive summary.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The infrastructure assessment identified additional middleware remediation requirements not captured in the original scope baseline. The estimated cost of this remediation is $340,000, representing ap

risks.description: The report's GREEN status and optimistic executive narrative actively contradict material issues buried in appendix notes (budget overrun, schedule slip, unresolved scope). This misalignment constitutes a governance and transparency risk, potentially leaving senior decision-makers uninformed and unable to take timely corrective action.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Status: GREEN ... No material risk escalations are required at this time. ... GC-Cloud Wave 4 is progressing excellently. The team has made strong progress across all workstreams.

risks.description: The infrastructure assessment revealed middleware remediation requirements that were not in the original scope baseline. This indicates the initial scope definition was incomplete. Additional undiscovered technical complexity may exist as the project moves into the implementation phase, risking further scope creep, cost escalation, and schedule delays.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The infrastructure assessment identified additional middleware remediation requirements not captured in the original scope baseline ... The findings from this assessment are being used to refine our c

risks.description: The infrastructure assessment phase slipped by approximately three weeks. While described as absorbed within contingency, this consumption of schedule buffer early in the project — before the implementation phase has begun — significantly reduces the team's ability to absorb future delays, threatening the Q3 2026 delivery commitment.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The infrastructure assessment phase required a slight adjustment of approximately three weeks from the original internal planning estimate, but this has been fully accommodated within the project's ex

risks.description: Procurement activities are still ongoing as the project prepares to enter the implementation phase in Q2 2026. Any delays in procurement — a process subject to Government of Canada regulatory requirements and vendor timelines — could directly compress or block the implementation schedule, jeopardizing the Q3 2026 delivery date.

evidence_refs.excerpt: The project team looks forward to entering the implementation phase in Q2 2026 following completion of the procurement activities.
